ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301737 covers flavored milk as a category; no strawberry-specific child term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers flavoured milk drinks as a category. Strawberry-specific sub-code not available; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 covers flavored milk broadly; no strawberry-specific descendant confirmed. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard single-serve carton of 200 ml, equivalent to approximately 206 g, as sold at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Milk, flavored, reduced fat, with added vitamin A and vitamin D (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 01211 adapted for whole-milk strawberry flavoured milk); cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food strawberry flavoured milk entries
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for flavoured whole milk (NDB 01211) and cross-referenced with multiple USDA Branded Food strawberry flavoured milk entries (e.g., FDC ID 1097512, 1097513). Values represent a consensus estimate for a standard commercial strawberry flavoured whole milk product at approximately 3.5% fat and 10–11% sugar content per 100 g. Per-100g figures are used; the 200 ml serving size is noted separately.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; flavoured milk GI reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 34 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for flavoured milk (chocolate milk GI ~34, strawberry milk GI ~37 per Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). A consensus value of 34 is used as a conservative estimate for strawberry flavoured milk, consistent with the low-GI profile of dairy-based flavoured milks. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (34 × 21.4) / 100 ≈ 7.3, where carbohydrate per 206 g serving = 10.4 × 2.06 ≈ 21.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (52 mg per 100 g; ~107 mg per 200 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single 200 ml serving contributes approximately 107 mg sodium, which is modest relative to the 2000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; however, individuals on strict sodium restriction should account for cumulative dairy intake across the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~10 g per 100 g; ~20 g per 200 ml serving) with low GI of 34
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite a low glycemic index of 34, the total sugar load of approximately 20 g per 200 ml serving is clinically significant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be counted within the daily carbohydrate budget; portion control and infrequent consumption are advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content (0.9 g per 100 g; ~1.9 g per 200 ml serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content per serving is low at approximately 1.9 g, well within the cardiovascular guideline threshold; the product is suitable for occasional consumption within a heart-healthy diet, though the added sugar content warrants moderation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Strawberry Flavoured Milk is a commercially produced, sweetened dairy beverage made from pasteurised whole or reduced-fat milk blended with strawberry flavouring, sugar, and stabilisers. It is sold in a standard 200 ml single-serve carton format and is widely available at quick-service restaurants and convenience outlets across Singapore, including Dunkin' Donuts. The drink has a sweet, mild strawberry taste with the creamy texture characteristic of dairy milk. It is ultra-processed and contains added sugars, contributing approximately 20 g of sugar per 200 ml serving. Nutritionally, it provides a moderate source of calcium (approximately 220 mg per serving), protein (approximately 6.6 g per serving), and B vitamins including riboflavin and B12, reflecting its dairy base. The glycemic index is low at approximately 34, consistent with other flavoured dairy milks, but the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 7.3 due to the total carbohydrate content. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor total sugar intake from this product; those managing cardiovascular disease will find the saturated fat content acceptable at approximately 1.9 g per serving. Sodium is modest at approximately 107 mg per serving, posing minimal concern for hypertension when consumed occasionally. The product is vegetarian, halal-suitable, and gluten-free, but contains dairy and is not suitable for lactose-intolerant individuals or vegans.
common_user_queries: How many calories in strawberry milk?
Is strawberry milk suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Branded Food entries for strawberry flavoured milk products; no exact Singapore-market product label data was available from an approved primary source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for flavoured dairy milks. Values are internally consistent and cross-referenced across multiple USDA entries but are not derived from a single exact product match.
notes: Serving size of 206 g is based on 200 ml volume with an approximate density of 1.03 g/ml for flavoured milk. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for commercial flavoured milk products; naturally zero by product formulation for added trans fats. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a liquid dairy beverage with no fibre-contributing ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central adapted values and are not -1 as they are reported in the source database. GI of 34 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for flavoured dairy milk; strawberry flavoured milk GI is consistent with chocolate milk GI values reported in Atkinson et al. 2008. The cuisine tag 'Coffee' from the input field survey record reflects the restaurant category (Dunkin' Donuts) rather than the food's actual cuisine classification; this has been corrected to 'Western / International'. No Singapore HPB Foodo entry was found for this specific product; USDA FoodData Central was used as the secondary global source per source policy.
